### Post by fate324 on 2008-03-02

the only resolution in System > preferences > resolution is 800x600 and 640x480

here is my /etc/X11/xorg.conf file

	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"

	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

	Driver		"wacom"

	Option		"Type"		"cursor"

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet

can anyone help?

---

### Post by Bri0 on 2008-03-02
Did you go into 'Restricted Drivers Manager' (System > Admin) and select?

Note: Also make sure in 'Software Sources' (System > Admin) that the 'Proprietary drivers for devices (restricted)' is checked.


If already did above and if that doesn't work, at the modes line..

Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"



Now have it like this (below), but change "1280x1024" to your resolution

Modes "1280x1024"



Save & Restart


This seems to work for me.

---

### Post by Yellow Pasque on 2008-03-02
Generate a modeline for the resolutions you need with cvt and paste it into your Monitor section. For example:
```
cvt -v 1280 1024 60.0Hz
```
Also use the virtual line right after SubSection "Display", i.e.:
```
SubSection "Display"
       Virtual   1280 1024
```

---
